description: §41.16.7 — when at least one `<column sortable>` slot is present, `rows=` must be a cell-reference expression (`rows=@<varName>`, with optional `.method()` chains) so the synthesized `@<varName>.sortedBy` cell has a stable name. A non-cell `rows=` (here a literal `[]` array) fires E-TABLEFOR-SORTABLE-REQUIRES-CELL-ROWS.
rationale: §41.16.7 E-TABLEFOR-SORTABLE-REQUIRES-CELL-ROWS: the sort surface synthesizes a state cell named after the rows source, which requires `rows=@<varName>`. Resolution: hoist the rows expression into a reactive cell (`<items> = ...` then `rows=@items`), OR remove the `sortable` attribute from the `<column>` slot if external sort is not needed.
